quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Hungarian word 'pillangó' (butterfly) comes from 'lepke', but the playful word literally means 'fluttering moment.' Hungary is home to over 160 species of butterflies, including the stunning Scarce Swallowtail that migrates through gardens each summer.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals in the Salish Sea
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals are year-round residents of the Strait of Juan de Fuca, hauling out on rocks near Port Angeles. Learn about their fascinating underwater communication and surprising intelligence.

medium_read.title: How Plants Know When to Bloom
medium_read.teaser: Scientists have discovered that plants use an internal clock and light sensors to time their flowers perfectly. This gentle explanation reveals the hidden intelligence in every garden.

long_read.title: The Lost Gardens of Budapest's Castle Hill
long_read.teaser: For centuries, terraced gardens graced Budapest's historic Castle District, filled with rare roses and medicinal herbs. Historians are now piecing together their stories and bringing some back to life.

port_angeles.title: Bald Eagles Nesting Along Ediz Hook
port_angeles.description: July is a wonderful time to spot bald eagle families along Ediz Hook and the waterfront. Adult eagles are teaching their fledglings to fish in the protected waters of the harbor. Bring binoculars for the best views!

puzzle_1.question: A ruby-throated hummingbird's heart beats about ___ times per minute when active.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! That tiny heart is working incredibly hard — like a little miracle of nature!
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! The answer is 1,200 beats per minute — those tiny hearts are amazing!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: The answer is 1,200 beats per minute! Can you imagine? That's about 20 beats every second!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the highest metabolism of all animals. They must visit hundreds of flowers each day just to get enough energy to survive.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'good morning'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful, Ildiko! Jó reggelt is exactly right — what a lovely way to greet the day!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The answer is 'jó reggelt'! Jó napot means 'good day,' jó estét is 'good evening,' and jó éjszakát means 'good night.'
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian greetings often include 'jó' (good) followed by the time of day. The formal 'reggelt' comes from 'reggel,' meaning morning.

puzzle_3.question: What type of tree is the most common in Olympic National Park?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Yes! Western Hemlock trees blanket the Olympic forests — they love the misty rain and grow so tall and graceful. You've probably enjoyed their shade on many walks!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's the Western Hemlock! These beautiful trees thrive in the Olympic Peninsula's rainy climate and can live for over 500 years. Their delicate needles and drooping tops make them easy to identify.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Western Hemlocks are the climax species of Pacific Northwest forests, meaning they're often the final tree to dominate a mature forest. Their tiny cones feed Douglas squirrels and many birds.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
